chapter_title: Articles of apparel and clothing accessories, not knitted or crocheted
scope_note: Note 1: Ch.62 applies only to MADE-UP articles of any textile fabric (other than wadding), EXCLUDING knitted or crocheted articles (except 62.12 brassieres/corsets/etc.). Note 9: left-over-right front closure = men's/boys'; right-over-left = women's/girls'; unidentifiable → women's/girls'. Note 10: articles may be made of metal thread.

inclusions.description: Men's or boys' overcoats, car coats, capes, cloaks, anoraks (including ski jackets), wind-cheaters, wind-jackets and similar articles, other than those of heading 62.03
Women's or girls' overcoats, car coats, capes, cloaks, anoraks (including ski jackets), wind-cheaters, wind-jackets and similar articles, other than those of heading 62.04

Men's or boys' suits, ensembles, jackets, blazers, trousers, bib and brace overalls, breeches and shorts (other than swimwear). Note 3(a): 'suit' = 2–3 pieces of identical fabric including a coat/jacket (≥4 panels, upper body) + lower garment (trousers/breeches/shorts/skirt — no braces/bibs). Includes morning dress, evening dress (tailcoat), dinner jacket suits. If multiple lower garments presented together → one pair trousers counts as the suit's lower part

Women's or girls' suits, ensembles, jackets, blazers, dresses, skirts, divided skirts, trousers, bib and brace overalls, breeches and shorts (other than swimwear). Note 3(b): 'ensemble' = several pieces of identical fabric (not suits or 62.07/62.08) comprising one upper garment + one or two lower garments of compatible size and style

Men's or boys' shirts. Note 4: 62.05 does NOT cover garments with pockets below the waist, ribbed waistband/tightening at bottom, OR sleeveless garments. 'Shirts' = upper body garments with long/short sleeves and full/partial opening from neckline

Women's or girls' blouses, shirts and shirt-blouses. Note 4: 62.06 does NOT cover garments with pockets below the waist or ribbed waistband/tightening at bottom. 'Blouses' may be sleeveless. 'Shirt-blouses' and 'blouses' may have a collar
Men's or boys' singlets and other vests, underpants, briefs, nightshirts, pyjamas, bathrobes, dressing gowns and similar articles
Women's or girls' singlets and other vests, slips, petticoats, briefs, panties, nightdresses, pyjamas, négligés, bathrobes, dressing gowns and similar articles

Babies' garments and clothing accessories. Note 5(a): 'babies'' = young children with body height ≤86 cm. Note 5(b): articles classifiable prima facie in both 62.09 and another Ch.62 heading → ALWAYS classify in 62.09
Garments made up of fabrics of heading 56.02, 56.03, 59.03, 59.06 or 59.07. Note 6: garments prima facie classifiable in both 62.10 and other Ch.62 headings (except 62.09) → classify in 62.10

Track suits, ski suits and swimwear; other garments. Note 7: 'ski suits' = overall (one-piece) or ski ensemble (anorak/windcheater with zipper + trousers/breeches, all same fabric texture/style/composition, compatible sizes)
Brassieres, girdles, corsets, braces, suspenders, garters and similar articles and parts thereof, whether or not knitted or crocheted
Handkerchiefs. Note 8: scarves/scarf-type articles square or approx. square with NO SIDE EXCEEDING 60 cm → classified as handkerchiefs (62.13)
Shawls, scarves, mufflers, mantillas, veils and the like. Note 8: handkerchiefs with ANY SIDE EXCEEDING 60 cm → 62.14
Other made up clothing accessories; parts of garments or of clothing accessories, other than those of heading 62.12

exclusions.condition: Knitted or crocheted articles of apparel (other than 62.12)
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 61 (knitted or crocheted clothing) — Note 1. Woven non-knit → Ch.62. Knit/crochet → Ch.61. Exception: 62.12 (brassieres, girdles, corsets) covers both knitted AND non-knitted

exclusions.condition: Worn clothing or other worn articles showing appreciable wear presented in bulk
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 63.09 — Note 2(a). Used/worn woven garments in bales → 63.09

exclusions.condition: Orthopaedic appliances, surgical belts, trusses and similar
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 90.21 — Note 2(b). Surgical abdominal belts, hernia trusses, orthopaedic supports even if made of textile → 90.21

classification_rules.rule: Note 1 — The knitted/woven split: Ch.61 vs Ch.62 is the fundamental divide for clothing
classification_rules.description: The primary classification decision for clothing: KNITTED or CROCHETED → Chapter 61. NOT knitted/crocheted (woven, non-woven, leather, etc.) → Chapter 62. This applies to all apparel and clothing accessories. Exception: heading 62.12 (brassieres, girdles, corsets, braces, garters) covers BOTH knitted and non-knitted forms — this heading spans both Ch.61 and Ch.62.

classification_rules.rule: Notes 5 and 6 — Special priority headings: 62.09 (babies') and 62.10 (technical fabrics) override other headings
classification_rules.description: Two headings have OVERRIDING PRIORITY within Ch.62: (1) Heading 62.09 = babies' garments (body height ≤86 cm) — Note 5(b): if prima facie classifiable in BOTH 62.09 and another Ch.62 heading → ALWAYS 62.09. (2) Heading 62.10 = garments of technical fabrics (56.02, 56.03, 59.03, 59.06, 59.07) — Note 6: if prima facie classifiable in 62.10 AND another Ch.62 heading (excluding 62.09) → classify in 62.10. Priority cascade: 62.09 (babies') > 62.10 (technical fabric) > other 62.xx headings.

classification_rules.rule: Note 3 — 'Suit' vs 'ensemble' definition: strict criteria for set classification
classification_rules.description: These define when multiple garments are classified as ONE ENTRY: SUIT (62.03/62.04): 2–3 pieces of IDENTICAL fabric (same construction, colour, composition, style); must include a coat/jacket with ≥4 outer panels + a lower garment (trousers/skirt/breeches — no braces/bibs). Multiple lower garments presented together → only ONE low garment is the suit component. ENSEMBLE (62.04): several pieces of identical fabric (same fabric construction, style, colour, composition, compatible size); one upper garment + 1–2 lower garments. Does NOT apply to track suits or ski suits (→ 62.11). If the garments don't meet the strict definition → each garment classified separately.

classification_rules.rule: Note 4 — Shirts (62.05) vs blouses (62.06) vs other upper garments: construction criteria
classification_rules.description: Note 4 excludes from 62.05 (men's shirts) AND 62.06 (women's blouses): garments with pockets BELOW the waist (those → 62.11 or other headings), garments with a RIBBED WAISTBAND or other tightening at the bottom. Additionally, heading 62.05 (men's shirts) does NOT cover SLEEVELESS garments. The definitions: 'shirt/shirt-blouse' = upper body, long/short sleeves, full/partial opening from neckline. 'Blouse' = loose-fitting upper body, may be sleeveless, with or without neckline opening.

classification_rules.rule: Note 8 — Handkerchiefs vs scarves: the 60 cm side-length test
classification_rules.description: Square or approximately square articles (scarves/shawls of any textile): if NO SIDE EXCEEDS 60 cm → classified as HANDKERCHIEFS (62.13). If ANY SIDE EXCEEDS 60 cm → classified as SHAWLS/SCARVES (62.14). This applies regardless of the article's intended use or how it is marketed. A 59×59 cm neck square → 62.13. A 61×61 cm square silk scarf → 62.14. Non-square scarves (rectangular) → 62.14 regardless of size.

classification_rules.rule: Note 9 — Men's vs women's garments: front closure direction rule
classification_rules.description: For garments that could be either sex: LEFT-OVER-RIGHT front closure → men's/boys'. RIGHT-OVER-LEFT front closure → women's/girls'. These provisions do NOT apply when the CUT or STYLE of the garment clearly indicates it is designed for one sex (e.g., a clearly feminine cut with right-over-left closure is women's regardless). Garments not identifiable as either sex → classified in WOMEN'S/GIRLS' headings (default). This affects subheading selection within 62.01–62.11.
